title: Fix internvl fp16 error
body:

files_modified.patch:
@@ -133,7 +133,11 @@ def _ipex_llm_load_model(self) -> None:
                     modules = ["vision_encoder", "embed_tokens_extend"]
                 if low_bit == "fp16":
                     # to fix qwen2.5-vl and glm-4v
-                    modules = ["vision", "visual"]
+                    if modules is None:
+                        modules = ["vision", "visual"]
+                    else:
+                        modules.append("vision")
+                        modules.append("visual")
                 optimize_model(self.model,
                                low_bit=low_bit,
                                torch_dtype=self.vllm_config.model_config.dtype,

steps.operation: Fix FP16 module selection logic by preserving any previously-determined module list and appending InternVL vision modules when needed
steps.reason: The prior implementation overwrote `modules` with `["vision", "visual"]` whenever `low_bit == "fp16"`, which could discard earlier module selections (e.g., model-specific lists) and lead to incorrect optimization coverage or failures for InternVL/Qwen2.5-VL/GLM-4V in FP16. The new logic only initializes the list if empty, otherwise extends it to include the required vision submodules.
steps.side_effects: FP16 optimization may now apply to a broader set of submodules (union of earlier selections plus `vision`/`visual`), potentially increasing optimization time or memory use; any downstream logic in `optimize_model(...)` that assumes `modules` is exclusive may behave differently due to the expanded list.

steps.operation: Adjust the arguments passed into `optimize_model(...)` by ensuring the `modules` list is correctly constructed before the call (no longer implicitly replaced for FP16)
steps.reason: `optimize_model(self.model, ...)` depends on the `modules` selection to decide which submodules to optimize/convert. By changing the FP16 branch to append rather than overwrite, the call receives the intended module set, preventing FP16 conversion errors for InternVL-related vision components while retaining other required modules.
steps.side_effects: Behavior of model conversion/optimization for FP16 may change for models that previously relied (intentionally or accidentally) on the overwrite; regression risk exists if some models should only optimize `vision`/`visual` in FP16, so model-specific validation may be required.

step_plan.summary: Fixes an FP16 conversion/optimization error for InternVL-family vision models by preventing the FP16 branch from overwriting the existing module selection and instead appending required vision submodules before optimization.
